overview.short: Bumzuva cave and temple are a group of Hindu temples and man-made caves in the village of Bumazuv in the Lidder Valley of Anantnag district in Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: Bumzuva cave and temple are a group of Hindu temples and man-made caves in the village of Bumazuv in the Lidder Valley of Anantnag district in Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Lidder Valley
topPlaces.description: The Lidder Valley or Liddar Valley is a Himalayan sub-valley of the Kashmir Valley, located in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The Lidder River flows down the valley.

topPlaces.name: Mattan, Jammu and Kashmir
topPlaces.description: Mattan (originally, Martand) is a town, tehsil and a notified area committee, near Anantnag city in the Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Martand Sun Temple
topPlaces.description: The Martand Sun Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mattan, in the Anantnag district of the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It dates back to the eighth century CE and was dedicated to Surya, the solar deity in Hinduism; Surya is also known by the Sanskrit-language synonym Martand (मार्तण्ड, Mārtaṇḍa).

topPlaces.name: Krewa Rampur
topPlaces.description: Krewa Rampur is a village in Anantnag tehsil in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Krewa Rampur village is located in Anantnag Tehsil of Anantnag district in Jammu & Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Manigam
topPlaces.description: Manigam is situated in Anantnag tehsil and located 1 kilometer from Seer Hamdan in the Anantnag district in the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is one of 105 villages in the Anantnag Block of Khovripora along with villages like Akad, Salia, Brar and Hutmarah.

topPlaces.name: Srigufwara
topPlaces.description: Srigufwara or Srigufwoar is a town and administrative sub-division, or tehsil, in the Anantnag district of the India-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It was given tehsil status by the government of Jammu and Kashmir on 1 February 2014.

topPlaces.name: Kanelwan
topPlaces.description: Kanelwan is a village in Bijbehara tehsil in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The population was 3,822 in the 2011 Indian census.

topPlaces.name: Chittisinghpura massacre
topPlaces.description: The Chittisinghpura massacre refers to the mass murder of 35 Sikh villagers on 20 March 2000 in the village of Chittisinghpura in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India on the eve of the U.S. President Bill Clinton's state visit to India. The Indian government asserts that the massacre was conducted by the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) or Hizbul Mujahideen.

items.note: The Lidder Valley or Liddar Valley is a Himalayan sub-valley of the Kashmir Valley, located in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The Lidder River flows down the valley.
Mattan (originally, Martand) is a town, tehsil and a notified area committee, near Anantnag city in the Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

The Martand Sun Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mattan, in the Anantnag district of the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It dates back to the eighth century CE and was dedicated to Surya, the solar deity in Hinduism; Surya is also known by the Sanskrit-language synonym Martand (मार्तण्ड, Mārtaṇḍa).
Krewa Rampur is a village in Anantnag tehsil in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Krewa Rampur village is located in Anantnag Tehsil of Anantnag district in Jammu & Kashmir.

Manigam is situated in Anantnag tehsil and located 1 kilometer from Seer Hamdan in the Anantnag district in the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is one of 105 villages in the Anantnag Block of Khovripora along with villages like Akad, Salia, Brar and Hutmarah.

Srigufwara or Srigufwoar is a town and administrative sub-division, or tehsil, in the Anantnag district of the India-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It was given tehsil status by the government of Jammu and Kashmir on 1 February 2014.
Kanelwan is a village in Bijbehara tehsil in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The population was 3,822 in the 2011 Indian census.

The Chittisinghpura massacre refers to the mass murder of 35 Sikh villagers on 20 March 2000 in the village of Chittisinghpura in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India on the eve of the U.S. President Bill Clinton's state visit to India. The Indian government asserts that the massacre was conducted by the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) or Hizbul Mujahideen.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bumzuva Cave and Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bumzuva Cave and Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bumzuva Cave and Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (91 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bumzuva Cave and Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bumzuva Cave and Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bumzuva Cave and Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bumzuva Cave and Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bumzuva Cave and Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lidder Valley, Mattan, Jammu and Kashmir, Martand Sun Temple, Krewa Rampur, Manigam.
